report_name: China-Linked APT MirrorFace Expands Targeting to EU Diplomatic Entity

threat_actors.name: MirrorFace
threat_actors.description: A China-aligned advanced persistent threat (APT) group focused on cyber-espionage. The group has historically targeted entities in Japan but was observed expanding its operations to a diplomatic organization in the European Union in April 2024. MirrorFace is known for using custom malware and legitimate tools like SoftEther VPN for persistence and C2.
threat_actors.goals: Intelligence gathering related to political and economic affairs.

attack_patterns.name: Protocol Tunneling
attack_patterns.description: The threat actor used SoftEther VPN to tunnel command and control traffic, making it difficult to distinguish from normal network activity.

attack_patterns.name: Application Layer Protocol: Web Protocols
attack_patterns.description: Command and control traffic, tunneled via SoftEther VPN, was disguised as normal HTTPS traffic.

tools.name: SoftEther VPN
tools.description: Leveraged by MirrorFace and other China-aligned groups to maintain stealthy, persistent access to compromised networks and for command and control (C2) communications.

identities.name: European Union
identities.description: A diplomatic organization within the European Union was targeted by the MirrorFace APT group, marking a shift from the group's traditional focus on Japan.

infrastructure.name: Unknown VPN Servers
infrastructure.description: SoftEther VPN was used to establish persistent connections to non-corporate VPN endpoints for command and control.

indicators.name: SoftEther VPN Client Execution
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: Unauthorized SoftEther VPN Client Execution
id: 2b1a9c8d-4e5f-4a3b-8c7d-6e5f4a3b2c1d
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of SoftEther VPN client processes, which could indicate unauthorized remote access by threat actors like MirrorFace.
references:
  - https://www.eset.com/int/about/newsroom/press-releases/research/eset-releases-latest-apt-report-china-aligned-groups-expand-targeting-iran-advances-diplomatic-esp/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-27
tags:
  - attack.t1219
  - attack.t1572
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\sevpn.exe'
      - '\sevpnclient.exe'
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate use of SoftEther VPN.
indicators.description: Detects the execution of SoftEther VPN client processes. The presence of these executables on workstations or servers where they are not authorized is a strong indicator of compromise, associated with groups like MirrorFace.

indicators.name: Suspicious LNK File in Startup Folder
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious LNK File Creation in Startup Folder
id: 9f8e7d6c-5b4a-4987-b6a5-4321fedcba98
status: experimental
description: Detects the creation of LNK files in user or system startup directories, a common persistence technique used by APTs.
references:
  - https://www.eset.com/int/about/newsroom/press-releases/research/eset-releases-latest-apt-report-china-aligned-groups-expand-targeting-iran-advances-diplomatic-esp/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-27
tags:
  - attack.t1547.009
  - attack.persistence
level: medium
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|endswith: '.lnk'
    TargetFilename|contains:
      - '\AppData\Roaming\Microsoft\Windows\Start Menu\Programs\Startup\'
      - '\ProgramData\Microsoft\Windows\Start Menu\Programs\StartUp\'
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate software installers creating startup shortcuts.
indicators.description: Detects the creation of LNK files in startup directories, a common persistence technique suggested for hunting MirrorFace activity.

indicators.name: Suspicious Registry Run Key Creation
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious Registry Run Key Creation
id: 1a2b3c4d-5e6f-7a8b-9c0d-1e2f3a4b5c6d
status: experimental
description: Detects modification of the registry run keys, a common persistence technique used by malware and APTs.
references:
  - https://www.eset.com/int/about/newsroom/press-releases/research/eset-releases-latest-apt-report-china-aligned-groups-expand-targeting-iran-advances-diplomatic-esp/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-27
tags:
  - attack.t1547.001
  - attack.persistence
level: medium
logsource:
  category: registry_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    TargetObject|contains:
      - 'Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run'
      - 'Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\RunOnce'
    EventType: 'SetValue'
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate software installers and updaters.
indicators.description: Detects the creation or modification of registry run keys for persistence, a hunting hint provided for detecting APT activity like MirrorFace.
